title: viola tricolor var. paillouxii
intro: Viola tricolor var. paillouxii, also known as Pailloux's pansy, is a variety of the heartsease or Johnny-jump-up, a flowering plant belonging to the violet family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and wounds, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Viola tricolor var. paillouxii include its potential to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation, as well as its ability to promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically to treat skin issues, such as eczema and acne.
